title: Twisha death case: Giribala Singh, Samarth to be sent to jail under judicial custody
content: A special CBI court in Bhopal is expected to send Giribala Singh, a former district judge, and her son Samarth Singh to judicial custody today. Both are accused in the dowry death case of model-turned-actor Twisha Sharma. The CBI did not seek further remand for the duo. The court's decision is anticipated this afternoon.

title: West Bengal BJP shuts doors on TMC rebels, vows to prevent 'Trinamoolisation'
content: Samik Bhattacharya, the West Bengal BJP leader, has taken a definitive stand against former TMC leaders who wish to join their ranks, branding them as 'tainted' and insisting that the BJP thrives on grassroots support. He also dismissed Mamata Banerjee's intentions to stage a protest over supposed post-poll violence as an attempt to sidestep her party's internal challenges.

title: Debt, inflation & IMF dependence: Pakistan still spending $900k every month on US lobbying
content: Pakistan is spending heavily on lobbying in the United States. This occurs as the nation faces economic difficulties and water scarcity. The country also deals with rising fuel costs. FARA filings reveal extensive efforts to influence Washington. This includes security, trade, and diplomatic outreach. The lobbying intensified during military tensions with India. Pakistan's army chief made claims about mediation.

title: 'Jeet gaye, kar diya na maine': Rajat Patidar's first words to childhood friend
content: Moments after RCB’s IPL 2026 victory, Patidar shared an emotional embrace with childhood friend Abhishek Pathrod, saying, “Dekha, jeet gaye. Kar diya na maine." The triumph capped a remarkable rise for the soft-spoken leader, whose calm captaincy and prolific batting helped transform RCB from perennial underachievers into back-to-back champions.

title: 'Isolate yourself': Govt advisory for travellers from Ebola-hit countries showing symptoms
content: India's Health Ministry has issued a travel advisory for those arriving from Ebola-hit nations, assuring no cases are currently reported domestically. Travelers from affected regions within the last 21 days experiencing symptoms like fever, vomiting, or bleeding are urged to self-isolate and contact health authorities immediately via the 24x7 helpline 1075 for guidance.

title: Microsoft tells engineers to stop using Anthropic's Claude
content: Microsoft is discontinuing most internal Claude Code licenses by June 30, directing engineers to its own GitHub Copilot CLI. This move follows Claude Code's unexpected popularity, which reportedly undercut Microsoft's homegrown product. While officially citing toolchain unification, the timing suggests cost-cutting amidst an industry-wide AI spending crunch.

title: 'Would not be a great idea': Manjrekar on sending Sooryavanshi overseas too soon
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive IPL 2026 performance, including 776 runs and 72 sixes, has ignited calls for his immediate promotion to India's T20I squad. While many former cricketers back the young talent, ex-player Sanjay Manjrekar urges caution, advocating for gradual exposure to international conditions and allowing his career to develop organically.

title: ED cracks down on Dawood aide in Maharashtra, Gujarat raids
content: Enforcement Directorate conducted extensive raids across Maharashtra and Gujarat, targeting over 20 locations in a money laundering probe linked to drug trafficker Mohammad Salim Dola. Dola, recently extradited from Türkiye, is accused of running a transnational drug syndicate and is a close associate of Dawood Ibrahim.

title: Iran war impact: RBI likely sold $12bn gold reserves to shield foreign currency assets
content: To limit the impact of these external shocks, the government has stepped up measures aimed at reducing foreign exchange outflows and stabilising the economy. These steps include increases in fuel prices and a sharp rise in import duties on precious metals.

title: Google plans to release 32mn Wolbachia-infected mosquitoes across Florida, California
content: Alphabet's Debug initiative is seeking EPA approval to release 32 million specially treated male mosquitoes in California and Florida. This innovative approach uses Wolbachia bacteria to sterilize wild Culex mosquitoes, aiming to significantly reduce the spread of diseases like West Nile virus. Advanced AI and robotics are enabling this large-scale effort, with trials planned over two years.

title: Virat Kohli shares 'Kikli' dance with Bhuvneshwar Kumar after RCB triumph - Watch
content: Virat Kohli and Bhuvneshwar Kumar celebrated Royal Challengers Bengaluru's second consecutive IPL title with a hilarious "Kikli" dance. Both veteran players were instrumental in RCB's dominant campaign, with Kohli leading the batting and Bhuvneshwar spearheading the bowling attack to secure their back-to-back championship wins.

title: Why Washington is considering expanding nuclear deployments across Europe
content: America is considering expanding its nuclear weapons deployment to more NATO allies, with eastern European nations like Poland reportedly showing interest. This move aims to bolster deterrence amid rising tensions with Russia. These arrangements, historically central to NATO's defense, ensure allies share in nuclear policy and risk, strengthening collective security and the US commitment to protect its partners.

title: India moves closer to mega Rafale deal, but will it solve the IAF's fighter shortage?
content: India is set to procure 114 Rafale fighter jets from France in a significant government-to-government deal worth Rs 3.25 lakh crore. While 18 will be fly-away, 96 will be manufactured in India. This acquisition aims to bolster the Indian Air Force's depleted squadron strength, though indigenous programs like Tejas Mk2 and AMCA are crucial for long-term security against evolving threats.

title: 'Peace sign' selfies create security scare on internet; experts explain 'danger'
content: Social media users are concerned about hackers stealing fingerprints from peace sign selfies, a fear amplified by viral posts. While experts confirm it's theoretically possible to extract fingerprints from high-resolution photos, the real-world risk for average users is minimal and less concerning than existing cyber threats like phishing.

title: Hardik Pandya to reach BCCI CoE on June 2 ahead of Afghanistan ODI's
content: Hardik Pandya is set to undergo fitness drills at the BCCI Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru from June 2, aiming for the ODI squad against Afghanistan. Meanwhile, the Test team begins training in Mullanpur for their one-off match. Auqib Nabi might be a late addition to the Test camp.

title: Rs 25 lakh theft at Raveena Tandon's brother's house; caretaker arrested
content: In a dramatic turn of events, Rashi Chhabria, a caregiver, has found herself in handcuffs for supposedly stealing jewellery worth ₹25 lakh from the home of Raveena Tandon’s brother in Mumbai. Entrusted with the care of the star's mother, Chhabria had cultivated the family's trust before the precious items disappeared from a secure location.

title: 'Mental torture': Punjab student dies by suicide; alleges harassment over unpaid fees
content: A 17-year-old student tragically ended her life after allegedly enduring severe mental harassment from her school over ₹20,000 in unpaid fees. Before her death, she recorded a viral video statement from her hospital bed, detailing the ordeal and holding the school administration responsible. Police have registered a case and are actively searching for the accused.

title: H-1B hiring boost: Nvidia offers top salaries despite slowdown in foreign recruitment
content: While many tech firms scale back, Nvidia is aggressively hiring foreign talent via the H-1B visa program, increasing its certified positions. This surge reflects the company's immense demand for AI specialists and engineers, contrasting with competitors like Google and Amazon. CEO Jensen Huang emphasizes immigration's vital role in US technological leadership.

title: Kelce caught in fresh Blake Lively–Taylor Swift drama rumors
content: Taylor Swift’s upcoming wedding to Travis Kelce has sparked renewed attention on some of her high-profile friendships. Reports claim Swift has grown distant from former close friend Karlie Kloss, while her relationship with Blake Lively remains under scrutiny following the actress’s legal dispute with Justin Baldoni. Sources suggest Swift has become more selective about her inner circle, prioritizing trust and long-term loyalty as she prepares for the next chapter of her life with Kelce.

title: Cowboys, Eagles Eye Blockbuster move for $160M Defensive Standout
content: The Dallas Cowboys and Philadelphia Eagles were among the teams linked to former Cleveland Browns star Myles Garrett before the Los Angeles Rams completed a blockbuster trade. Cleveland received rising pass rusher Jared Verse, a 2027 first-round pick, and additional draft assets. Garrett, whose contract is worth roughly $160 million, is expected to receive a new deal in Los Angeles as the Rams strengthen their championship hopes.

title: FEMA case: ED conducts searches against Vedanta Group
content: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has initiated searches against the Vedanta Group as part of a Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) investigation. The action against the mining conglomerate, promoted by Anil Agarwal, commenced on Monday. Further details regarding the ongoing FEMA probe are yet to be disclosed by the central agency.

title: Lalit Modi backs Sooryavanshi to become 'bigger than any Hollywood and Bollywood star'
content: Lalit Modi has predicted a meteoric rise for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who was the leading run-scorer in IPL 2026. Modi believes the Rajasthan Royals prodigy could score a T20 double century and become a global superstar, surpassing even Hollywood and Bollywood actors.

title: Fabricated hospital records, fake lab reports: Delhi court rules in favour of insurance company
content: A Delhi consumer court confirmed the insurer's denial of a health claim, revealing serious discrepancies in medical documentation. Investigators uncovered that a patient had been administered an injection after their discharge, with evidence showing a pathologist's signature was forged.

title: TOI's IPL team of the season: Sooryavanshi-Kohli at the top; Patidar named captain
content: In the sensational IPL-2026 season, young talent Vaibhav Sooryavanshi took the spotlight, smashing records left and right. Not to be outdone, the legendary Virat Kohli proved that age is just a number with his phenomenal performances on the field. Meanwhile, Rajat Patidar steered RCB to triumph, securing a remarkable second title.

title: Punjab drug smugglers throw cash to foil police chase, held after encounter
content: A dramatic chase in Sonbhadra led to the arrest of a Punjab-based drug smuggler, Prince, who was shot in the leg. He and his accomplice attempted to evade police by throwing cash and ramming vehicles. Recovered items include 3 kg opium, 225 kg doda, and Rs 50,000 cash, valued at Rs 58.75 lakh. His accomplice escaped.

title: Salman Khan's legal team files case against 'Kala Hiran', demands film be pulled
content: Salman Khan's legal team has sent a notice to the makers of 'Kala Hiran', a film based on the blackbuck poaching case, demanding they halt its release and remove all promotional material, warning of further legal action if their demands are not met.

title: Op Checkmate: 30 Indians held for illegally working as truck drivers in US, deportation likely
content: US Border Patrol's Operation Checkmate has led to the arrest of approximately 30 Indian nationals working illegally as commercial truck drivers in Arizona. These individuals, along with others from different countries, are facing deportation. The operation aimed to enhance public safety by enforcing immigration laws and identifying undocumented drivers operating heavy vehicles.

title: 'Never imitated anyone': When Suman Kalyanpur spoke on comparision with Lata Mangeshkar
content: On May 31, 2026, the music world mourned the loss of Suman Kalyanpur, a veteran singer often celebrated for her voice reminiscent of the legendary Lata Mangeshkar. Her remarkable journey in playback singing was marked by grace and talent, as she bravely created her own distinctive musical identity.

title: House collapses in Delhi's Mukundpur after LPG blast; several feared trapped
content: An LPG cylinder blast caused a single-storey house to collapse in Delhi's Mukundpur area on Tuesday morning. Several residents are feared trapped under the debris. Rescue teams were immediately dispatched to the scene to launch operations following the incident.

title: Ivanka assassination plot: Accused had blueprint of her home, wanted to 'burn down' Trump's house
content: An IRGC-linked operative, Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, pleaded not guilty to terrorism charges in Manhattan, accused of plotting to assassinate Ivanka Trump and orchestrating global attacks. He claimed innocence, stating, "Our children are being killed by your rockets." The indictment details alleged bombings and planned attacks, with potential life sentences.

title: Rajat Patidar gives Krunal Pandya the ultimate 'Aura' tribute
content: Krunal Pandya was instrumental in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's back-to-back IPL title wins in 2025 and 2026. He delivered crucial performances throughout the season, contributing significantly with both bat and ball. Captain Rajat Patidar acknowledged his vital role in the team's historic success.

title: 'Saw potential in a young worker': DKS thanks Gandhi family ahead of swearing-in
content: Karnataka CM-designate DK Shivakumar expressed deep gratitude to the Gandhi family for their pivotal role in his political career. He vowed to work with sincerity and commitment to build a progressive, inclusive, and prosperous Karnataka, drawing inspiration from Rajiv Gandhi, Sonia Gandhi, Rahul Gandhi, and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra.

title: Bhopal horror: Man's 'friends' share drinks with him, then rape and stab his wife
content: A woman was allegedly molested, stabbed, and robbed by two friends of her husband after they dropped him home intoxicated. The accused followed the wife inside her residence, assaulted her, and stole her mangalsutra before fleeing. Police have arrested both individuals and they have been sent to jail.

title: South Korea overtakes India to become world’s sixth largest stock market; here’s why
content: In the cases of both South Korea and Taiwan, the stock market rally is fuelled by a powerful surge in semiconductor giants that are benefiting from the global artificial intelligence boom.

title: MasterChef winner Pankaj Bhadouria raises breast cancer awareness amid diagnosis
content: MasterChef winner Pankaj Bhadouria shared an emotional health update after successful cancer surgery, expressing deep gratitude for widespread support. She urged women, especially those in their 40s, to prioritize regular breast cancer screenings and preventive health check-ups, emphasizing early detection's crucial role in recovery.

title: Russia launches major attack across Ukraine; several feared trapped after missile-hit building collapses
content: Russia unleashed a major overnight assault on Ukrainian cities, including Kyiv and Dnipro, hours after President Zelenskyy's warning. Fatalities and injuries were reported in Dnipro, with fears of people trapped in Kyiv's rubble. Meanwhile, a Ukrainian drone strike claimed a civilian life in Russia's Kursk region. Peace efforts remain stalled amidst escalating cross-border attacks.

title: CUET UG 2026 admit cards released for rescheduled exams: Check link to download hall tickets here
content: The National Testing Agency has released updated admit cards for CUET (UG) 2026 candidates whose exams were rescheduled due to a technical glitch on 30 May 2026. The affected examinations will now be held on 06–07 June 2026. Candidates are required to download their revised hall tickets from the official portal and follow all instructions carefully.

title: Only 3 players have achieved rare IPL three-peat - and 2 are from RCB
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured their second IPL title, defeating Gujarat Titans. Amidst the celebrations, Phil Salt and Suyash Sharma achieved a rare IPL title hat-trick, winning three consecutive trophies with Kolkata Knight Riders and then RCB. They join Karn Sharma as the only players to achieve this remarkable feat.

title: No Instagram, no TikTok till 16: Malaysia bans social media accounts for teens
content: Malaysia has banned children under 16 from social media, requiring platforms to verify ages and remove underage users. This move aims to protect minors from harmful content and cyberbullying. While some parents support the restrictions, others worry about unintended consequences and the potential for children to bypass the rules.

title: Quote of the day by Mark Ruffalo
content: Actor Mark Ruffalo, honored for environmental activism, shared a powerful message at Dickinson College: embracing the unknown fuels personal and artistic growth. He urged stepping outside comfort zones, facing fears like failure, and taking leaps of faith to foster resilience and achieve goals. Ruffalo's insights encourage bold action, emphasizing that growth stems from venturing into uncharted territory.

title: Tharoor questions full rendition of Vande Mataram, calls it 'unnecessary imposition'
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor questioned the mandatory full recitation of Vande Mataram at every public event, deeming it an "unnecessary imposition." He emphasized that while the national song commands respect, forcing its complete rendition twice during short programs is difficult to justify.

title: Fire breaks out at steel plant in Chhattisgarh, no casualties
content: The fire department contained the blaze within a few hours and the police have launched a probe to assess the extent of the property damage

title: 27 people from Ebola-affected nations under home quarantine in Kerala
content: All passengers from Ebola-affected nations — Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda and South Sudan   — entering the State are being made to undergo mandatory quarantine of 21 days from their arrival in Kerala

title: Kerala Assembly: LDF walks out, accuses UDF of neglecting fuel crisis
content: Leader of Opposition Pinarayi Vijayan says CM Satheesan ‘skimmed over the pain’ ordinary households endure due to supply chain chaos caused by US-Iran war by ‘offhandedly replying’ that government will ‘examine the matter’

title: ED raids in Maharashtra, Gujarat in Salim Dola drugs case
content: The 59-year-old Dola was brought back by India from Türkiye in April and was arrested by the Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) soon after he landed at the Delhi airport

title: Does so-called fatherland mean more than his motherland: Congress' dig at PM on Israeli offensive in Lebanon
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh said the negotiations continue between the U.S. and Iran to bring a halt to the war in West Asia

title: Suspicious drone over Meerut Cantonment puts security agencies on alert; probe on
content: According to officials, information was received on the evening of May 30 that some suspicious camera-equipped drones were seen flying over the cantonment area

title: ‘Assault’ on family in Kerala’s Vagamon rekindles tourist safety concerns
content: A video of the alleged assault, which surfaced on social media on Monday night, has since gone viral and sparked widespread outrage

title: CBSE re-evaluation portal goes live after delays; students report glitches
content: CBSE’s re-evaluation portal went live after a delay but faced immediate complaints from students over login failures and technical glitches, following earlier concerns about the board’s new evaluation system.

title: Kurnool police arrest 18 over vandalism during RCB IPL win celebrations
content: Eighteen people are arrested after RCB’s IPL win celebrations turned to vandalism of government and private property in Kurnool

title: Mehbooba Mufti writes to J&K leaders, calls for Ladakh-like united outreach to PM, HM
content: She urged regional leaders “to keep disagreements and contrarian views on the back burner to unite for the common good and collective welfare”

title: Congress has again capitulated to Muslim League on Vande Mataram: BJP
content: BJP’s allegation came after Shashi Tharoor questioned the need to play all five verses of Vande Mataram at the beginning and end of official functions, describing the practice as an "unnecessary imposition"

title: Kerala govt to provide land, houses for seven SC families facing eviction at Malayidomthuruth, says CM Satheesan
content: Replying to a calling attention motion, CM says the government is trying to solve the issue involving two private parties amicably

title: Arava Sreedhar resigns as Government Whip; resignation accepted with immediate effect
content: The Jana Sena legislator cites personal reasons; the move follows allegations by a woman government employee and a police case against him

title: In Odisha, one in five women face domestic violence; one in four children stunted: NFHS-6
content: One in five women face partner violence and one in four children are stunted in Odisha, according to the latest NFHS-6 survey, which also flags low female economic participation and a steep rise in C-section deliveries as key concerns for the State

title: Kerala govt to launch special drive in Athirappilly to tackle rogue elephants posing threat
content: Forest Minister Shibu Baby John announces surveillance, fencing upgrades and compensation revision after high-level review

title: Five new Supreme Court judges sworn in, strength increases to 37
content: The appointments come shortly after the Union Government increased the court’s sanctioned strength from 33 to 37 judges, excluding the CJI; the latest round of elevations is also expected to balance regional representation and enhance gender diversity on the Bench

title: Karnataka changes alcohol pricing | India’s first alcohol-based tax system explained
content: Karnataka has become the first state in the country to implement the Alcohol in Beverage (AIB) excise duty structure.

title: Trinamool Congress panchayat chief found dead at home in West Bengal
content: The deceased has been identified as Jahidul Haque Baidya, the chief of Jadurhati Uttar Gram panchayat in Baduria area

title: The silken voice of Hindi cinema falls silent
content: From vibrant chartbusters to melancholic ghazals, the versatile singer ruled hearts without chasing the spotlight

title: Enforcement Directorate searches against Vedanta Group in FEMA case
content: Vedanta spokesperson said “We are extending full cooperation to the authorities and are providing all information sought”

title: ‘From Raaja With Love’: Maestro Ilaiyaraaja puts on a mesmerising show in Chennai, despite glitches
content: After playing his symphony ‘Valiant’ on Saturday, the ‘king of music’ greeted music lovers with a unique set of tracks, though the performance was marred by technical issues

title: The Hindu Huddle on Campus to be held at Amity University in Bengaluru on June 3
content: The Hindu Huddle on Campus is an initiative to create spaces for dialogue, debate, and critical thinking among the youth

title: CM envisions transforming Telangana into global gateway
content: At Telangana Formation Day celebrations, Chief Minister Revanth Reddy spoke about Telangana Rising – 2047, welfare schemes, initiatives to financially empower women and job notifications

title: Kerala woman assaulted for questioning eve-teasing
content: The woman, accompanied by her friend, was returning to their room in Kochi after a part-time job around 4 a.m. when they were allegedly subjected to eve-teasing by a group, which also included two women

title: CBSE opens verification and re-evaluation portal
content: CBSE said the portal was live during the early hours on Tuesday (June 2, 2026), missing its original deadline of June 1, 2026; several users on X complained that they were unable to login

title: Maggots in patient’s surgical wound: Kerala Health Minister orders inquiry
content: Complaint was raised by family of Rajendraprasad of Paripally, who was admitted to Thiruvananthapuram MCH following a road accident in which he sustained serious injuries to legs

title: I came to know about my brother’s death only through Google search: Chamba accident victim’s sister
content: A tourist taxi carrying seven visitors from Bengaluru and a local driver is feared to have plunged into a deep gorge along the treacherous Bairagarh-Sach Pass-Killar road in the remote Churah subdivision of Himachal Pradesh’s Chamba district on the night of May 29, leaving all eight occupants feared dead

title: Bellatrix Aerospace to help launch South Korean VLEO satellite mission
content: Under the partnership, the TelePIX’s high-resolution wide-swath optical payload Chouette will be integrated with the Bellatrix Aerospace’s VLEO satellite platform powered by Air-Breathing Electric Propulsion (ABEP)

title: Shanimol Usman elected Kerala Deputy Speaker
content: UDF candidate Shanimol Usman won the contest with 99 votes. Her opponent, CPI leader and LDF candidate Muhammed Muhassin, secured 34 votes

title: Why do Indian cities get polluted in summer? | Explained
content: How is summer air pollution different from that in winter? Why does ozone rise in hot weather? What drives PM10 spikes in Indian cities? How do dust storms affect air quality? How do human activities worsen summer air pollution? What can cities do to combat summer air pollution?

title: Representatives from 11 countries to take part in BRICS meet in Odisha’s Puri from June 3-5
content: India had chaired the first technical meeting of the BRICS Disaster Risk Reduction Working Group, held virtually in April, to advance a shared agenda on disaster resilience

title: Breakaway faction of banned Jamaat rallies behind J&K L-G Manoj Sinha’s anti-drug campaign in volatile Kulgam
content: “When we choke off drug smuggling, we weaken those who seek to destabilise our nation, spill the blood of innocent people, and divert our youth from the path of progress,” says the Lieutenant Governor

title: Public prosecutor caught red-handed while taking ₹5,000 bribe in Bengaluru
content: The accused was allegedly demanding a bribe to submit objections in a case registered at the Chennamanakere Achukattu Police Station

title: 388 cells on 2 floors, this Kerala cemetery beats space crunch
content: Structure in Thrissur was designed to make use of the available space without compromising on the requirements and by addressing environmental concerns

title: Deepak Cables: ED seizes ₹1.27 crore cash and gold, freezes ₹18 crore in ₹899-crore bank fraud case in Bengaluru
content: The money laundering investigation stems from a CBI case registered against the DCIL, and others, for allegedly cheating a consortium of banks led by the State Bank of India of ₹899.35 crore

title: Sri Lankan Tamil girl killed after gang rams SUV into two-wheeler on Chennai’s Koyambedu flyover
content: A six-member gang first picked a fight with her and then assaulted her before driving the SUV into her two-wheeler, say the police

title: Purported audio of Minister attempting ‘sabotage’ of Davanagere bypoll goes viral
content: The Davangere bypoll preparations had turned tricky for the Congress leadership amid rumours that sections of Muslims, upset over denial of ticket to a candidate from the community, might turn against the party

title: After partial collapse, push to restore Radhakrishnan heritage site in Mysuru
content: Located in Saraswathipuram, the bungalow served as residence of former President Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan when he taught philosophy at the University of Mysore

title: ‘Outsiders’ lobbying to enter Rajya Sabha from Karnataka Congress
content: Y.S. Sharmila, Supriya Shrinate, and Pawan Khera from outside Karnataka are said to be lobbying with the Congress leadership for a Rajya Sabha berth from the State

title: Sharmila slams TDP for criticising Y.S. Rajasekhar Reddy, Y.S. Raja Reddy
content: Andhra Pradesh Congress Committee president asks why the TDP did not act against the two leaders when it was earlier in power, and accuses the ruling coalition of ‘cheap politics’

title: Telangana statehood Day: Committed to supporting State’s growth trajectory, says PM Modi
content: Established on June 2, 2014, it became India’s 29th state after being carved out of the northwestern region of Andhra Pradesh

title: Undertrial prisoners entitled to be treated at private hospitals of their choice: Madras High Court
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan permit Devanathan Yadav of Mylapore Hindu Permanent Fund Nidhi Limited to undergo treatment at a private hospital for 10 weeks

title: Prisons in India continue to be overcrowded by undertrials | Data
content: More than half of the States/UTs had an occupancy rate of over 100% in 2024 despite a modest increase in capacity

title: The Iran war and the uncertain future of the expats
content: The Gulf will continue to matter for India, but the old certainty of the “Gulf dream” has been shaken

title: West Asia crisis clouds monsoon tourism hopes of Kumarakom
content: Stakeholders say there is no influx of Arab travellers to the backwater destination; industry pins hopes on travellers from north India for its revival

title: Twelve years of dedication, a legacy of achievement
content: The fact that Narendra Modi’s Prime Ministership and his government have been in place for 12 years is a direct indication of the public’s steadfast faith in him

title: "Destroy His Authority": Secret Texts Reveal How UK PM Lost Labour Control
content: McFadden warned that if the government lost the vote, withdrew the bill, or significantly watered down the reforms, it would "destroy" Starmer's authority as Prime Minister.

title: 51 Wounds, Cigarette Burns: Kerala Toddler Abused For A Month, Killed By Stepfather
content: On May 29, when the child cried loudly, the man struck him on the head, and destroyed evidence before taking the toddler to the hospital.

title: Taiwan Tests Robot Dogs That Could Soon Guard South China Sea Islands
content: The Taiwan military's top weapons development institute showed off on Tuesday three robot patrol dogs that could one day be used on Taiwan's islands in the disputed South

title: Annamalai, Vijay, And A Brewing Rivalry That Mirrors MGR vs Karunanidhi
content: Sources indicate Annamalai's proposed political platform could be built around "Tamil nationalism and governance."

title: US-Iran War Hits Real Estate: Construction Costs Jump 25%, Delays Loom
content: US-Iran War: As supply shortages shift demand toward domestic manufacturers, local suppliers are also revising prices more frequently.

title: Srinagar Airport To Remain Closed Twice A Week From July To September
content: Srinagar Airport suspends flights on Mondays and Tuesdays for runway maintenance from July to September.

title: Woman Recovers After Cashless Heart Treatment Under Punjab Government Scheme
content: Launched by the Bhagwant Mann government, the Mukh Mantri Sehat Yojana provides eligible families with cashless medical treatment worth up to Rs 10 lakh annually.

title: Team Trump Wants 'Neutral' Oman To 'Pick A Side' That's Not Iran
content: The Trump administration's distrust of Muscat reportedly started a day before the first US-Israeli airstrikes when Oman's foreign minister claimed an agreement between Washington and Tehran on nuclear issues was "within our reach".

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Officer Says Renewed War With US 'Inevitable' Amid Trump's Peace Push
content: US-Iran war live updates: The remark by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf came as reports suggested US President Donald Trump has sent back a revised, tougher peace proposal

content: President Donald Trump is an enthusiastic advocate for swift AI development in America, dismissing regulation as a curb...

content: The Enforcement Directorate has reportedly asked that the role of CPM leaders in the incident also be examined.

content: There is an 80-percent chance of the warming El Nino phenomenon developing between June and August, increasing the risk of extreme weather events, the...

title: India Tweaks Solar Rules To Cut China Dependence, Manufacturers Divided
content: Manufacturers say modules built using domestic cells are substantially more expensive than those using imported cells.

title: Nvidia's Big H-1B Push Is Silver Lining For Indian Techies In US
content: Nvidia Jobs: Nvidia has secured certification for around 1,200 H-1B positions during the first two quarters of fiscal 2026.

title: All Eyes On Ritabrata Banerjee As Rebellion Brews Against Trinamool
content: Ritabrata Banerjee and Sandipan Saha were expelled by Trinamool yesterday over alleged anti-party activities.

content: A Vedanta spokesperson confirmed the ED action and said the company was "extending full cooperation to the authorities".

title: Fans Damage Buses During RCB Celebrations, 18 Arrested In Andhra, Hyderabad
content: The police have arrested 18 people for allegedly damaging public and private property during celebrations.

content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India.
